name: My Mom's Extraordinary Chocolate Chip Cake
recipeInstructions:
Preheat the oven to 325 degrees. Butter an 11x7x1-3/4 inches baking pan.
In a large bowl, mix together the flour, brown sugar, and cinnamon for 15 seconds on low speed.
Add the butter and mix until the pieces are pea-sized, about 1 minute. You will see loose flour in the mixture.
Add the egg. The mixture will still look dry.
Mix in the baking soda into the sour cream.
Stir together the sour cream mixture and milk (if used) with the batter until evenly moistened.
Stir in the chocolate chips. The batter will be thick.
Spread the batter evenly in the prepared pan.
Bake for about 40 minutes. Test for doneness by inserting a toothpick; it should come out clean with a few crumbs attached.
Cool the cake completely on a wire rack before serving. Note that the center of the cake may sink slightly after cooling.
To freeze, cut the cake into two or three large pieces or 20 individual pieces, remove from the pan, and wrap in plastic wrap followed by heavy aluminum foil. Label with date and contents before freezing. For larger pieces, stack them in the freezer afterward.
